content: Just as intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM) and random network distillation (RND) reward reinforcement learning agents for exploring uncertain state space to reduce prediction error, the open-ended iterative search for stable agent definitions in AI research itself acts as a collective curiosity-driven reinforcement learning loop, where competing ambiguous definitions of "agent" function as the intrinsic reward for exploring uncharted methodological territory. This framing redefines unresolved debate around the core concept of an "agent" as a feature, not a bug, of AI research's intrinsic reward structure.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
